values.ParentCommandLine: "C:\Windows\System32\cmd.exe"  /c bcdedit /set {default} bootstatuspolicy ignoreallfailures & bcdedit /set {default} recoveryenabled no & wbadmin delete catalog -quiet & wmic shadowcopy delete

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Suspicious Copy From or To System Directory
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description:
Detects a suspicious copy operation that tries to copy a program from system (System32, SysWOW64, WinSxS) directories to another on disk.
Often used to move LOLBINs such as 'certutil' or 'desktopimgdownldr' to a different location with a different name in order to bypass detections based on locations.
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Markus Neis, Tim Shelton (HAWK.IO), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)

values.CommandLine: "C:\Windows\System32\cmd.exe"  /c bcdedit /set {default} bootstatuspolicy ignoreallfailures & bcdedit /set {default} recoveryenabled no & wbadmin delete catalog -quiet & wmic shadowcopy delete
C:\Windows\System32\cmd.exe  /c bcdedit /set {default} bootstatuspolicy ignoreallfailures & bcdedit /set {default} recoveryenabled no & wbadmin delete catalog -quiet & wmic shadowcopy delete

crowdsourced_yara_results.description: Detects command variations typically used by ransomware
crowdsourced_yara_results.author: ditekSHen

crowdsourced_yara_results.description: Detects command variations typically used by ransomware
crowdsourced_yara_results.author: ditekSHen

crowdsourced_ai_results.analysis: The sample is a Nephilim ransomware variant. It disables system recovery and deletes backups by executing commands via `cmd.exe`, including `bcdedit /set {default} recoveryenabled no`, `wbadmin delete catalog -quiet`, and `wmic shadowcopy delete`. It then traverses all fixed, removable, and network drives, encrypting files using a hybrid cryptographic scheme. Encrypted files are renamed with a `.NEPHILIN` extension. A ransom note named `NEPHILIN-DECRYPT.txt` is dropped in directories, and the desktop wallpaper is changed to a ransom message. The malware ensures single-instance execution via the mutex `sofos delaet sosos`.
